project.name: SOCIAL.md
project.one_liner: Every product deserves a launch. We all have been there. Great idea, all night of hacking, and then what - project goes to trash! SOCIAL.md is an open protocol that gives every project a structured, agent-readable launch plan so your side-project, library, or startup gets the distribution it deserves.
evidence: Live launch page describes SOCIAL.md as a non-profit, open protocol and Social Agent Protocol.
Launch page includes concrete spec primitives: Manifest, Channels, Assets, Tasks, Templates, Agents.
README states the core founder insight: hackathon projects often die after build because distribution is not systematized.
assumptions: Raise amount and runway are draft planning numbers and need founder confirmation.
Legal entity and financing instrument are not yet specified in the repository.

team.headline: Founder-led builders with direct pain insight from hackathon-to-distribution workflows.
bullets: Shipped end-to-end narrative quickly: live launch page, protocol framing, and implementation-ready examples.
Strong technical execution signal: automation skills and scripts for launch operations exist in this repository.
Community-first orientation matches open protocol adoption dynamics for developer ecosystems.
evidence: Git history shows active build progression with 5 commits and latest commit date 2026-02-21.
Repository includes working automation artifacts: LinkedIn CLI skill and Slidev pitchdeck generation script.
assumptions: Founder bios, prior exits, and role ownership need to be added for investor diligence.
Planned hiring timeline (engineering, GTM, developer relations) is not yet documented.

problem.headline: Great products still fail because launch distribution is ad hoc and repeatability is missing.
bullets: Builders spend energy shipping product, then run out of time to market while launch windows expire.
Current workflow is usually one-off posting with no channel plan, no audience map, and no checklist.
Each project relaunches from zero, so there is no compounding launch knowledge across projects.
Agents cannot help effectively when launch context is unstructured.
evidence: Launch page problem section explicitly lists: no time to market, no distribution strategy, no launch checklist, and agents cannot help.
README idea statement centers on post-hackathon drop-off as the motivating pain point.
assumptions: Economic cost of missed launches (lost users, jobs, or revenue) still needs quantified case studies.

solution.headline: SOCIAL.md standardizes launch context so humans and agents can execute distribution reliably.
bullets: Add one SOCIAL.md file in repo root with project metadata, audiences, channels, assets, and tasks.
Agents and automation tooling parse the same file to draft posts, generate assets, and track execution.
Reusable templates turn launch from improvisation into repeatable operating workflow.
Open protocol approach enables broad ecosystem integrations without platform lock-in.
evidence: Launch page includes full SOCIAL.md examples and a 3-step workflow: Add file -> Agents read it -> Launch with confidence.
The Spec section defines six protocol concepts with explicit structure and intent.
assumptions: Cross-platform publishing integrations need production-grade adapters and reliability SLAs.
Ecosystem interoperability depends on third-party agent/tool adoption over time.

why_now.headline: Agent-native workflows are mainstream enough that a launch protocol can become default infrastructure.
bullets: AI agents are now part of developer workflows, making machine-readable launch context immediately useful.
Distribution channels are fragmented; teams need one canonical source of launch truth.
Open-source and indie product creation velocity keeps increasing, amplifying post-build distribution pain.
A protocol-first standard can create network effects as templates and integrations compound.
evidence: Launch page positions SOCIAL.md as agent-readable and community-maintained protocol infrastructure.
Repository itself is organized around AI-agent-oriented skills and automation scripts.
assumptions: API and policy stability across social platforms may affect automation breadth.
Protocol adoption flywheel (contributors -> templates -> integrations -> users) requires active community operations.

traction.headline: Early proof is execution speed and concrete protocol articulation, with user validation as the next milestone.
bullets: Live launch page already communicates end-user pain, protocol spec, examples, and contribution path.
Product positioning is clear across three initial segments: hackathon projects, OSS libraries, and student startups.
Core tooling exists to support downstream launch workflow elements (pitch creation and social automation).
Immediate next step: convert current interest into design partners and measurable launch outcomes.
evidence: Launch page has dedicated Examples and Community sections with segment-specific use cases.
Recent repository activity indicates ongoing implementation and iteration.
assumptions: No public metrics are provided yet for waitlist, activation, retained users, or paid conversions.
Need to run first 10-20 design-partner launches and capture before/after distribution outcomes.

market.headline: Start with underserved builder segments, then expand into broader launch operations infrastructure.
bullets: Beachhead: hackathon builders, OSS maintainers, and student founders who lack formal GTM support.
Expansion: indie SaaS teams, developer tools startups, accelerators, and product education programs.
Monetization path: hosted automation workflows, premium templates, team collaboration, and analytics/reporting.
Protocol strategy can create defensibility through ecosystem standardization and workflow lock-in.
evidence: Website examples directly map to three concrete initial customer archetypes.
Spec primitives support multi-channel launches for many project categories.
assumptions: Bottom-up TAM/SAM/SOM model and pricing sensitivity by segment still need primary research.
Enterprise and accelerator distribution channels are unvalidated at this stage.

ask.headline: Raise to validate repeatable user outcomes and ship protocol + automation v1.
milestones: Publish SOCIAL.md v1 spec and reference validator with versioning governance.
Complete 20 design-partner launches with documented distribution uplift and playbooks.
Ship first-party integrations for core channels and launch analytics dashboard.
Reach repeatable weekly activation and retention targets for builder cohorts.
use_of_funds: 30% growth and developer relations (community, onboarding, playbooks).
15% operations and legal (governance, compliance, core infrastructure).
bullets: Use capital to move from narrative traction to measurable launch outcomes.
Prioritize protocol quality, onboarding velocity, and design-partner conversion.
Prepare a strong seed narrative based on validated activation and retention signals.
evidence: Current artifacts support early-stage conviction but not yet outcome metrics.
Open protocol framing is already public and can support contributor-led expansion.
assumptions: Raise size and milestone sequencing should be aligned with target investor profile.
Milestone metrics and conversion thresholds need founder calibration before fundraising outreach.
